manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: australia, goat, watermelon, lion, korea, iran, bear, plum, moose.
Your opponent's team words are: mosquito, hotel, flood, clouds, mansion, wasp, shack, cottage.
Distractor words are: whisk, missile, barbie, spatula, machete, blocks, yoyo.
The kill words are: parakeet.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a clue that relates to some of our team's words, can be any number. Must be single word, not any from list or containing parts. Provide CLUE and TARGETS list (comma-separated). Avoid kill words etc. Need to be careful not to hint opponent/distractor words.

Our team words: australia, goat, watermelon, lion, korea, iran, bear, plum, moose.

We need a clue that ties to several. Perhaps "wild". That could relate to lion, bear, moose, goat (wild animals). But also "wild" might associate with "wasp"? Not in opponent list, but disaster? Not kill. Could also be "north"? Not relevant. "Cold" maybe bear, moose, lion? Not lion. "Asia"? Relates to korea, iran, maybe also plum (origin Asia), but also "mosquito"? Not opponent? Mosquito not? Could be "asia" clue but "korea", "iran" and "plum"? Actually plum originated in China, so Asia. Also "goat"? Not. "Australia" is not Asia. So maybe "continent". That could hint Australia, Korea, Iran. But also maybe "goat"? No. "bear"? No. Might be too ambiguous.

Better to choose clue that covers as many as possible but safe. Perhaps "Safari". That evokes lion, bear? Actually safari includes lion, maybe moose? Not. "Forest" maybe bear, moose, goat, plum (grow). Watermelon? Not. "Fruit" could be watermelon, plum. But also "goat"? Not. "Zodiac" could be lion (Leo), goat (Capricorn), maybe bear (Ursa). Could also be "plum"? Not. But "zodiac" might hint opponent? No.

We must avoid linking to opponent words: mosquito, hotel, flood, clouds, mansion, wasp, shack, cottage. Also distractor words: whisk, missile, barbie, spatula, machete, blocks, yoyo.

Our clue should not be any of those.

"Zodiac": opponent words not related. Distractors not related. Good. Target words: lion (Leo), goat (Capricorn). Bear? Ursa major not zodiac. Moose not. Plum not. Watermelon not. Iran, Korea, Australia not. Could also be "Leo" but clue cannot be lion because that's a target word. So zodiac might be okay.

Maybe clue "Capricorn" but that's a goat. That's a target directly; clue cannot be any part of target? It cannot be one of the words in the lists (including target words). Capricorns not listed, okay. But "Capricorn" relates to goat (zodiac sign). Not to others.

Better to try "Savanna": relates lion, maybe bear? Not. "Koala"? Not target. "Kangaroo" not target but clue "downunder"? That hints Australia. But also could hint "goat"? No.

Maybe choose "Bears" is target. Can't.

Goal is to maximize. Could we combine "Asia" - relates to Korea, Iran, plum (origin), maybe also bear? The giant panda is Asia but not a bear? Actually panda is bear. That's possible but may be too indirect.

"Cold" could hint moose, bear, maybe also lion? Not.

Maybe pick two targets like "fruit". Relates watermelon, plum. Good. No opponent mis-association with fruit? Wasps like fruit? But not direct. Could be "hotel"? No.

Clue "fruit": opponent words: no fruit. Distractors: none. Kill word: parakeet no.

Thus we can give TARGETS: watermelon, plum.

We could also include "goat" if "fruit" not.

Maybe "wild" includes lion, bear, moose, goat. That's four. Opponent words: "wild" could relate to mosquito (wild insects). Maybe but guesser might not guess mosquito because it's opponent word. But they might not think of mosquito. Could be risky but okay. Better to keep lower.

Let's do "safari": lion, bear? Bear not typical safari. Moose maybe in some safaris? Not typical. Might be ambiguous.

I think "fruit" is safe. Provide clue:

CLUE: fruit
TARGETS: watermelon, plum

That's short
